description_long: This is a large, roughly cuboidal stone block with irregular, jagged edges and a stratified, layered appearance. The surface is predominantly off-white with subtle beige and brown streaks, suggesting natural mineral veining and weathering. The block features several planar faces, some of which are fractured or chipped, giving it a rugged, quarried look. Fine cracks and fissures are visible, especially along the edges and corners, indicating exposure to the elements. The texture is coarse and uneven, with visible grain and roughness typical of sedimentary or metamorphic stone. There are no visible engravings, markings, or artificial modifications, and the block appears solid and heavy.
description: A large, rugged off-white stone block with layered, jagged surfaces and natural brown veining. Its coarse, fractured texture and irregular shape suggest it was quarried and exposed to weathering.
description_view_0: This view highlights the block's top and two adjacent sides, showing pronounced layering, rough fractures, and subtle brown mineral streaks.
description_view_1: This side view reveals a relatively flat, broad face with visible stratification and some horizontal fissures, emphasizing the block's thickness.
description_view_2: This angle shows the underside and a corner, with deep recesses and pronounced layering, as well as more intense brown discoloration.
description_view_3: This view displays a mostly solid, less fractured face with a smoother surface and lighter coloration, with some weathered edges.
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set directly on compacted ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible minor chipping and surface discoloration, with some fine cracks and brownish streaks indicating exposure to rain and dirt.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or loosely grouped with other blocks
